friction_points.summary: No price shown on activation; Skeptical Investors assume hidden charge
Bottom-modal popup interrupts and is dismissed before read
Countdown timer perceived as manufactured urgency, alienates Skeptical Investors
Outline 'Unlock FREE trade' vs sticky '₹1 Trial' perceived as label mismatch / mildly deceptive
friction_points.notes: Element-level: 33% of V4 users noticed the discrepancy. 5/50 flagged it as friction.

friction_points.summary: Bright green CTA perceived as budget / reduces premium feel; penalizes Trust Seekers
friction_points.notes: Trust Seeker conversion dropped V2 50% -> V3 40%, attributed to this change (V2->V3 differs only in cta_style).

friction_points.summary: Blurred trade card reads as a gimmick to Skeptical Investors
Abstract performance claims fail where named past wins (V1) succeeded
friction_points.notes: V1 was the only variant with trust_signal=regulatory_plus_evidence + evidence_detail=named_past_outcome; dropping both in V2+ is the mechanism.

friction_points.summary: Low-contrast CTA missed entirely; banner mistapped
friction_points.notes: Source states 22% (= 11/50) of users either missed the CTA entirely or tapped the banner mistakenly.

citations.quote: Trial without a price means hidden charge
Show me a real trade or don't
I still don't believe the 84.7% claim, but ₹1 with a refund is essentially free
citations.context: price + refund framing — note: V4 has refund_or_guarantee_copy=absent per source; this quote may be cross-variant reflection or a source inconsistency

citations.quote: I bought it last year — if they nailed that, I should listen
Best of both worlds — looks premium and tells me the price
citations.context: V4 reception — source annotates n=48 which does not match Trust Seeker segment size n=10; treated as aggregate/cross-segment note

element_level_notes.note: 71% cited a stock by name (Recent Wins effect)
64% cited refund clause as strongest pricing reassurance

confounds.note: Crown header, blurred card, and implicit refund copy co-occur exclusively in V2/V3. V4 drops crown_header and refund copy but retains blurred_card, partially breaking this cluster.
Refund framing in price_visibility and refund_or_guarantee_copy co-vary perfectly — cannot attribute independently. They may be two names for the same on-screen element.
High-contrast green only appears with dual stack in V4; V3 has green with single stack, which breaks the cluster. Isolated attribution possible via V3 vs V4 contrast.

In Univest data, SEBI presence and named-wins evidence appear together only in V1 and nowhere else. Cannot attribute Curious Beginner's 27pt Control->V1 lift to one vs the other from data alone. See improvements.md: split trust_signal.

clean_element_contrasts.observation: Only cta_style changed. Bargain Hunter +16pts (46 -> 62). Trust Seeker -10pts (50 -> 40). Skeptical +9pts (8 -> 17). Curious +7pts (20 -> 27).
clean_element_contrasts.inference: high_contrast_green helps conversion-focused segments; penalizes premium-sensitive Trust Seeker.

clean_element_contrasts.observation: Overall completion +16pts; friction count drops from 3 to 2 (price opacity + modal interrupt resolved; countdown introduced).
clean_element_contrasts.inference: Cannot isolate single-element contribution without more variants — confounded change set.
Confounded change set — attribution requires decomposing via friction data (dual-CTA mismatch at 33% notice rate suggests dual_stack is net positive despite friction).

extraction_confidence._note: inferred = not stated explicitly in source; derived from adjacent evidence. Downstream synthesis must not treat inferred values as directly sourced.

flags: Trust Seeker quote attributed to n=48 in source; segment size is n=10. Treated as aggregate observation, not segment-bound.

Skeptical Investor V4 quote mentions 'refund' but V4's refund_or_guarantee_copy is 'absent' per source variant description. Possible source inconsistency or quote refers to ₹1 price framing rather than explicit refund copy. Flagged, not resolved. See tasks/improvements.md: confirm V4.refund via UI screenshot.

Conversion rates are rounded percentages (8%, 17%, 25%). Given small n per segment (10-15), the true uncertainty band is ±5-10 percentage points. Downstream synthesis should not overfit to 1-2pt differences.
V2/V3 cta_primary_label is inferred from the 'Activate @ ₹1' banner, not from an explicitly stated button label. See extraction_confidence.
